goal: Add a Multiply(a, b int) int function to main.go and add a corresponding test in main_test.go.
summary: Inspect the existing Go files to align with current package and test structure, add a new Multiply(a, b int) int function in main.go that returns the product of its inputs, then add or extend a unit test in main_test.go to verify correct multiplication behavior. Finish by running the Go test suite to confirm the new function compiles and the test passes without introducing unrelated changes.
product_scope: Update main.go to define Multiply(a, b int) int.
Update main_test.go to add a unit test covering Multiply.
Verify the implementation by running Go tests in the workspace.
non_goals: Changing existing public behavior beyond adding Multiply.
Adding extra math helpers or broader test coverage not needed for Multiply.
Modifying files outside main.go and main_test.go unless required by the build.
proposed_steps: Inspect main.go and main_test.go to confirm package name, existing functions, and current test style.
Add Multiply(a, b int) int to main.go with logic that returns a * b.
Add a unit test in main_test.go that asserts Multiply returns the expected product for a representative case.
Run go test ./... from the workspace and confirm the new test passes.
Do a final check that only the intended files were changed and acceptance criteria are met.
success_signals: The codebase compiles with the new Multiply function present.
The full Go test command exits successfully.
Diff shows only the expected changes in main.go and main_test.go.

verification_contract.goal: Verify that Multiply(a, b int) int was added to main.go, that main_test.go contains a working test for it, and that the Go test suite passes.
required_checks: Confirm main.go defines Multiply(a, b int) int.
Confirm Multiply returns the product using a * b.
Confirm main_test.go includes a test that calls Multiply and checks the expected result.
Confirm go test ./... succeeds from the workspace.
disallowed_actions: Do not change existing behavior unrelated to adding Multiply and its test.
verification_contract.notes: Keep the change minimal and consistent with the existing package and test conventions.
